purpose: Publish a bounded machine-readable map of which public surfaces become stale under which triggers so reviewers and maintainers can assess summary freshness without inferring it from raw git history alone.

surfaces.summary: Repo-shape summaries and release counts should refresh whenever files, catalog membership, or verification coverage change.
stale_signals: manifest coverage no longer matches repo files
repo-local check count no longer matches package scripts
reviewer-facing counts lag behind current release evidence

surfaces.summary: Reviewer shortcut layers should refresh whenever fast-review paths, strongest evidence, or stronger-source hierarchy change.
stale_signals: reviewer docs mention fewer summary layers than the catalog publishes
evaluation packet omits newer summary surfaces
summary layers outlive the release evidence they summarize

surfaces.summary: API, schema, and example freshness should follow contract and route changes, not only prose updates.
refresh_when: public examples gain or lose coverage
stale_signals: examples still teach deprecated or changed payload shapes
example-coverage summaries no longer match public fixtures
API docs imply behavior no longer backed by current schemas

surfaces.summary: Governance summaries should refresh when change posture, sync groups, or stronger-source authority moves.
refresh_when: sync groups gain or lose required surfaces
artifact authority or stronger-source hierarchy changes
stale_signals: release sync expectations no longer mention current governance summaries
ownership or freshness summaries disagree about which surfaces outrank others
maintainer docs describe refresh discipline not reflected in machine-readable governance layers

surfaces.summary: Audience paths and adoption-readiness summaries should refresh when ready vs deferred expectations change.
stale_signals: audience paths imply a readiness level not reflected in adoption posture
deferred hosted-runtime expectations disappear from reviewer-facing materials
limitations and adoption summaries disagree about what is intentionally out of scope

surfaces.summary: Freshness summaries should remain tied to evidence timeline and release evidence instead of drifting into static folklore.
stale_signals: timeline shows ongoing hardening but freshness expectations remain static
release metadata carries new risks without corresponding freshness updates
freshness docs fail to explain how summary layers age relative to release evidence
